core_content.markdown:
3. (Singapore)4 Let f be a polynomial of degree n, such that $\begin{array}{r}{f(k)=\frac{k}{k+1}}\end{array}$ for every integer $0\leq k\leq n$ . Compute $f(n+1)$ 1

4.$(\mathrm{U S A})^{5}$ Let f be a real polynomial of degree n, such that $f(k)={\binom{n+1}{k}}^{-1}$ for $0\leq k\leq n$ .Compute all lof 司$f(n+1)$ 公司

5.$\mathrm{(I M O\mathrm{\mathrm{—}s h o r t l i s t})}$ A polynomialf,of degree 990, is such that $f(k)=F_{k}$ for 992$\leq k\leq1982$ ,where $F_{k}$ stands for the k-th Fibonacci number. Prove that 

$f(1983)=F_{1983}-1$ 



6. (IMO—shortlist) Let f be a real polynomial such that 

(a)

$$f(1)>f(0)>0.$$

(b)

$$\widehat{f(2)}>2f(1)-f(0).$$

(c)

$$\widehat{f(3)}>3f(2)-3f(1)+f(0).$$

(d)

$$f(n+4)>4f(n+3)-6f(n+2)+4f(n+1)-f(n)$$

$$n\in\mathbb{N}$$

Prove that $f(n)>0$ for every $n\in\mathbb{N}$
